one_line: Custom-ASIC fundamentals are still growing >100% YoY and guidance was reiterated not cut, but the stock's 12-13% post-earnings drop plus a real competitive crack (Google picked MediaTek over Broadcom for TPUv9 'Triggerfish') mark the shift from euphoric re-rating to a 'prove it every quarter' phase, now compounded by a sector-wide valuation unwind (weak June payrolls, Burry shorting SOXX/NVDA/AMAT on 'beginning of the end' overvaluation thesis).
earnings_facts: Broadcom reported Q2 FY2026 (fiscal quarter ended ~May 2026, reported 2026-06-03/04): revenue $22.19B (beat consensus $22.13B), EPS $2.44 (beat $2.39). AI semiconductor revenue $10.8B in Q2, up 143% YoY. Q3 AI semi guidance: $16.0B, up >200% YoY but BELOW analyst estimate of $17.2B. CEO Hock Tan reiterated (did not raise) full-year AI semiconductor revenue guidance of 'in excess of $100 billion' for FY2027. Stock fell ~12-13% (worst single session in over a year) on 2026-06-04, driven by the unraised guidance and Q3 miss vs. Street, not by an actual earnings miss -- 'the bar was really high going into the print' (CFRA's Angelo Zino, quoted in the Yahoo Finance/CNBC coverage). Overall consolidated revenue growth remains strong (+48% YoY in Q2; Q3 total revenue guided +84% YoY to $29.4B), so this reads as expectations-reset, not a fundamentals miss.
catalysts: Positive: OpenAI's first custom AI chip ('Jalapeno') built with Broadcom; broadening custom-ASIC customer base (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) still active; hyperscaler AI capex estimated ~$650B for the year keeps demand pipeline large; consensus still Buy-rated (26 analysts, ~$524 12-mo target per July 1 snapshot). Negative/watch: Google selected MediaTek (not Broadcom or Qualcomm) as exclusive builder for its next TPUv9 'Triggerfish' variant (production ~late 2027, volume ramp 2028) -- first real evidence of a hyperscaler diversifying a core TPU program away from Broadcom, per Seeking Alpha/GF Securities commentary, though analysts there call it 'short-term noise' relative to Broadcom's broader AI-foundry position. Macro overhang: global AI-stock selloff extending into 2026-07-02 after a weak June jobs report; Michael Burry disclosed (2026-07-01) fresh shorts in NVDA, AMAT, CAT, PLTR, TSLA and the SOXX semiconductor ETF (which includes AVGO), calling chip-sector valuations 'a historically high extension' not seen since 2000 -- a sector-wide multiple-compression risk rather than an AVGO-specific order cut. Watch next: whether Q3 AI semi guidance ($16B) is met/beaten, and whether Broadcom announces new hyperscaler design wins to offset the Google/MediaTek TPUv9 loss.

citations.fact: Q2 revenue $22.19B/EPS $2.44 beat; Q3 AI chip guidance $16B vs. $17.2B street estimate; CEO reiterated (not raised) >$100B FY2027 AI semi revenue guidance; stock fell ~12% on the guidance miss despite the earnings beat; hyperscaler AI capex ~$650B cited.

Michael Burry disclosed new shorts in NVDA, AMAT, CAT, PLTR, TSLA and moved/expanded SOXX semiconductor ETF puts (Jan 2027 to Mar 2027, strikes raised to >$400); called Philly Semiconductor Index extension above its 200-day MA 'historically high,' unseen since 2000; AVGO not named directly but is a SOXX component.
